red_baseline.reason: T025 owns ordered routing and forbidden-call evaluations; the canonical Skill behavior update belongs to T026.
red_baseline.summary: 6/8 pass; only the missing canonical trace block and five executable force/risky-clean routes fail, exactly at T026's Skill-content boundary.

validation.summary: 6/8 pass; the missing trace contract and existing public force/risky-clean command spans are the only intentional failures.
Syntax check passes for 100 JavaScript files and whitespace validation reports no errors.

review.evidence: Fixtures assert ordered calls rather than final prose, exactly one healthy mutation, pre-dispatch-only CLI fallback, no fallback after refusal/uncertainty, bounded journal recovery, and zero-call refusal for all dropped intents.

obligation_evidence.evidence: The deterministic trace begins with lifecycle intent gating and requires MCP-first routing.
The trace model makes business refusal and post-dispatch uncertainty terminal for surface fallback.
Traces reference only the shared MCP/CLI semantic operations and no Skill-owned state or service lifetime.
Known and lost response traces permit only get/list/reconcile after dispatch.
Forbidden semantic operations and raw command/cwd/env/force fields are rejected by fixture scans.
No host approval or model-authored approval input exists in any trace.
Medium-risk intent has a zero-call refusal trace.
Confirmation-shaped input keys are absent and forbidden.
confirmed/yes/approvalToken are recursively excluded from trace inputs.
Indeterminate work routes to journal recovery without repeating the original handler.
adoption.apply is forbidden and its intent ends without calls.
Zero and ambiguous correlation traces stop unresolved without retry.
Assertions operate on machine-readable ordered traces and executable code spans, not plausible prose.

recovery: The first run used a Skill URL one directory above the repository. Both test paths were corrected and the original command rerun before accepting the intentional RED.
